LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS GARY FLOYD AND HEIDI KNODLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE REMAINS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THERE IS DEADLOCK OR DISSENSION OF A DEGREE REQUIRING A COURT TO EXERCISE ITS EQUITABLE POWER TO ORDER DISSOLUTION AND, IN ANY EVENT, THERE WOULD REMAIN ISSUES AS TO THE TERMS OF ANY SUCH DISSOLUTION. THE COURT IS NOT PREPARED TO RULE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE CONSEQUENCES PROPOSED BY THE LLC AGREEMENT ON A MEMBER PETITIONING TO DISSOLVE AN LLC ARE NECESSARILY UNLAWFUL. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
